segments.text: Alright hustlers and founders, stop crying over broken spreadsheets! Today, we are generating a pristine, professional GST invoice using Zoho Invoice.
segments.subtitle: Step 1: Open Zoho Invoice - free online invoicing tool for businesses

segments.text: The best part? It's free and needs zero logins. No passwords to forget today. Fire up the free generator page and let's get this bread.
segments.fallback_text: Jump into the free generator. No login needed.
segments.subtitle: Step 2: Open the free invoice generator page - no login required

segments.text: Boom, we're in. First up, who's getting paid? We are. Type in your company name, TechStar Solutions, your name, and address.
segments.subtitle: Step 3: Enter business details: company name, contact, and address

segments.text: And now we take a deep breath. The internet is doing its magic. Hydrate. Stare intensely at the screen... and we're good!
segments.fallback_text: One moment while the form catches up.
segments.subtitle: And now we take a deep breath. The internet is doing its magic. Hydrate. Stare intensely at the screen... and we're good!

segments.text: Time to bill the big fish. Enter the client details: Acme Corp International. Drop in their address and lock in the billing state, because the taxman is always watching.
segments.fallback_text: Now fill in the client details.
segments.subtitle: Step 4: Enter Bill To details for Acme Corp International

segments.text: Set that invoice number to INV-2026-001. The first invoice is always the sweetest.
segments.subtitle: Step 5: Set invoice number to INV-2026-001

segments.text: Now for the fun part. Line item one: Web Development Services. Forty units at twenty-five hundred a pop. Don't forget the 9 percent GST.
segments.subtitle: Step 6: Add line item 1 with quantity, rate, and GST

segments.text: Line item two: UI/UX Design, twenty units at three thousand.
segments.subtitle: Step 7: Add line item 2: UI/UX Design services

segments.text: Now we let Zoho do the heavy lifting. We are not here for mental math.
segments.fallback_text: Zoho is handling the math for us.
segments.subtitle: Now we let Zoho do the heavy lifting. We are not here for mental math.

segments.text: One more for the road! Line item three: Annual Server Hosting. Eighteen thousand flat.
segments.subtitle: Step 8: Add line item 3: annual server hosting

segments.text: Quick pause while the totals update. Like a slot machine spin, except you actually win every time.
segments.fallback_text: Quick pause while the totals refresh.
segments.subtitle: Quick pause while the totals update. Like a slot machine spin, except you actually win every time.

segments.text: Almost home. Drop your payment terms in the notes: payment due within 30 days. Add your bank details so the money actually finds its way to you.
segments.fallback_text: Add the payment terms and bank details.
segments.subtitle: Step 9: Add payment notes and terms for the client

segments.text: Final sanity check. Subtotal, GST, grand total looking absolutely crispy.
segments.subtitle: Step 10: Review subtotal, GST breakdown, and grand total

segments.text: Hit that Download button, grab your PDF, and boom!
segments.fallback_text: Download the invoice as a PDF.
segments.subtitle: Step 11: Use Download/Print to export the invoice as PDF

segments.text: Flawless GST invoice in under two minutes. Now go get your money!
segments.fallback_text: And that's a wrap for Overpass.
segments.subtitle: Step 12: Tutorial complete - create professional GST invoices in minutes
